project.name: ruvector slice 5b — Postgres extension + Cluster coordinator
project.description: ruvector-postgres: High-performance PostgreSQL vector database extension v2 - pgvector drop-in replacement with 230+ SQL functions, SIMD acceleration, Flash Attention, GNN layers, hybrid search, multi-tenancy, self-healing, and self-learning capabilities ruvector-cluster: Distributed clustering and sharding for ruvector. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the ruvector-cluster crate providing distributed clustering and sharding atop ruvector-core, with tokio/dashmap/async-trait dependencies and research-tier lint relaxations.
nodes.languageNotes: Workspace-inherited fields plus per-crate lint allow-list documenting deliberate lint downgrades for a research-tier crate.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build producing a Postgres image with the pgrx-based RuVector extension preinstalled, compiling the Rust extension and bundling required SQL/runtime assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage build separates the heavy Rust+pgrx toolchain from the slim Postgres runtime image.

nodes.name: index_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite measuring HNSW/IVFFlat index build, search, recall, latency percentiles, parallel scan, and SIMD distance-metric performance for the Postgres extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Criterion's `criterion_group!`/`criterion_main!` with clustered-vector generators to drive realistic ANN workloads.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Operator-facing guide for running RuVector inside Docker, covering image variants, environment variables, persistent volumes, and the integration-test harness shipped alongside the extension.

nodes.name: run-integration-tests.sh
nodes.summary: Bash harness that spins up the dockerised Postgres+RuVector image, runs the SQL integration test corpus, and reports pass/fail with cleanup hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: 18 helper functions provide colored logging, container lifecycle, and SQL fixture orchestration.

nodes.name: GNN_USAGE_EXAMPLES.md
nodes.summary: Practical SQL usage examples for invoking the GraphSAGE/GNN layers exposed by the extension, with end-to-end recipes for node embedding and neighbor aggregation queries.

nodes.name: ROUTING_QUICK_REFERENCE.md
nodes.summary: Cheat-sheet for RuVector's query routing layer, summarizing routing rules, function signatures, and tuning knobs that direct queries to the right index strategy.

nodes.name: ATTENTION_QUICK_REFERENCE.md
nodes.summary: Concise reference for the attention-mechanism SQL surface (scaled-dot, multi-head, flash), listing function arity, complexity classes, and recommended use cases.

nodes.name: 04-hyperbolic-embeddings.md
nodes.summary: Design and integration plan for hyperbolic (Poincare/Lorentz) embedding support inside the Postgres extension, covering math, SQL surface, and rollout phases.

nodes.name: ruvector.conf.template
nodes.summary: Postgres GUC template enumerating tunable RuVector parameters (SIMD mode, memory pooling, parallelism, cache sizes) intended to be included from postgresql.conf.

nodes.name: embeddings.sql
nodes.summary: SQL definitions binding the extension's fastembed-backed embedding generation API (ruvector_embed, batch, model load/unload, stats) to C wrappers exposed via MODULE_PATHNAME.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Top-level attention module that enumerates supported attention variants (scaled-dot, multi-head, flash) and provides shared softmax/normalization utilities used by the SQL-exposed attention operators.

nodes.name: AttentionType
nodes.summary: Enum tagging the available attention algorithm families with helpers for name/complexity/best-fit lookup used by the routing layer.

nodes.name: Attention
nodes.summary: Configuration wrapper for an attention operator, bundling type, head count, and dimensional metadata used when building per-query attention pipelines.

nodes.name: softmax
nodes.summary: Stable softmax over a slice of logits returning a new owned vector, shared by all attention variants.

nodes.name: softmax_inplace
nodes.summary: Allocation-free softmax that normalizes a mutable slice in place, used in the hot path of flash and scaled-dot attention.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the DAG-related SQL functions (analysis, attention, config, qudag, status) so they can be registered as a single pgrx surface.

nodes.name: scalar.rs
nodes.summary: Scalar (non-SIMD) implementations of vector distance metrics — euclidean, cosine, hamming, jaccard, minkowski, canberra, bray-curtis — used as the portable fallback path for the extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Portable f32 implementations match the SIMD variants byte-for-byte so they double as correctness oracles.

nodes.name: euclidean_distance
nodes.summary: Computes the L2 distance between two equal-length f32 slices.

nodes.name: euclidean_distance_squared
nodes.summary: Returns the squared L2 distance, avoiding the sqrt for ranking-only comparisons.

nodes.name: cosine_distance
nodes.summary: Computes 1 - cosine similarity with explicit zero-norm guards for ANN search.

nodes.name: hamming_distance_f32
nodes.summary: Counts component-wise mismatches between two f32 vectors using a small epsilon for float equality.

nodes.name: jaccard_distance
nodes.summary: Jaccard distance computed via min/max sums suitable for sparse-like f32 vectors.

nodes.name: minkowski_distance
nodes.summary: Generalized Lp distance parameterized by p, the unifying form of Euclidean and Manhattan.

nodes.name: canberra_distance
nodes.summary: Canberra distance weighted by absolute magnitudes, robust to scale differences.

nodes.name: bray_curtis_distance
nodes.summary: Bray-Curtis dissimilarity used for compositional/abundance vectors.

nodes.name: graphsage.rs
nodes.summary: GraphSAGE layer implementation with neighbor sampling, configurable aggregators, and linear-transform/forward routines that back the GNN SQL functions in the extension.

nodes.name: GraphSAGELayer
nodes.summary: Trainable GraphSAGE layer with mean/pool/lstm aggregators, neighbor sampling, and stochastic-gradient update support.

nodes.name: with_aggregator
nodes.summary: Constructs a GraphSAGELayer for a given aggregator variant and dimensionality, initializing weight matrices.

nodes.name: sample_neighbors
nodes.summary: Uniformly samples up to fanout neighbors from a node's adjacency list with replacement when sparse.

nodes.name: linear_transform
nodes.summary: Applies the layer's weight matrix to a concatenated feature vector to produce the next-layer embedding.

nodes.name: forward_with_sampling
nodes.summary: End-to-end GraphSAGE forward pass: samples neighbors, aggregates, concatenates self+neighbor, and applies the linear transform plus activation.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Applies an SGD-style weight update from a gradient and learning rate.

nodes.name: ast.rs
nodes.summary: Complete AST type hierarchy for the embedded SPARQL dialect: queries, graph patterns, RDF terms, expressions, aggregates, solution modifiers, and update operations parsed and lowered by the graph subsystem.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Rust enums with associated data to model the full algebraic surface of SPARQL 1.1.

nodes.name: SparqlQuery
nodes.summary: Top-level SPARQL query AST node combining the form (SELECT/CONSTRUCT/ASK/DESCRIBE), dataset, where pattern, and solution modifiers.

nodes.name: GraphPattern
nodes.summary: Enum of SPARQL graph patterns (BGP, Join, Optional, Union, Filter, Bind, Minus, Service, Graph) forming the algebraic core of query evaluation.

nodes.name: PropertyPath
nodes.summary: Property-path enum covering predicate, inverse, sequence, alternative, and Kleene-star operators used in BGP traversal.

nodes.name: RdfTerm
nodes.summary: RDF term type spanning IRIs, blank nodes, and literals with helpers for term-kind classification.

nodes.name: Expression
nodes.summary: SPARQL expression enum covering literals, variables, arithmetic, comparison, logical, function calls, and built-ins (REGEX, BOUND, etc).

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.summary: SPARQL aggregate function set (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, GROUP_CONCAT, SAMPLE) with DISTINCT flagging.

nodes.name: UpdateOperation
nodes.summary: SPARQL Update operation enum (INSERT, DELETE, LOAD, CLEAR, CREATE, DROP, COPY, MOVE, ADD) covering the full update surface.

nodes.name: functions.rs
nodes.summary: pgrx-exported SQL functions exposing the self-healing subsystem — history, manual trigger, strategy configuration, threshold tuning, and per-problem-type controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Each `ruvector_healing_*` function is wired to Postgres via pgrx attribute macros (#[pg_extern]).

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_history
nodes.summary: Returns the recent healing-event history as a SETOF row for SQL inspection.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_history_since
nodes.summary: Filtered healing-history reader returning events newer than a given timestamp.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_history_for_strategy
nodes.summary: Returns healing-history rows scoped to a specific recovery strategy.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_trigger
nodes.summary: Manually triggers the healing subsystem for a given problem type, returning the chosen strategy.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_execute
nodes.summary: Executes a healing strategy by name and returns success/diagnostic metadata.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_configure
nodes.summary: Updates healing configuration parameters (enable flags, cooldown, max retries) from SQL with validation.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_get_config
nodes.summary: Returns the current healing configuration as a JSON document.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_strategies
nodes.summary: Lists available healing strategies with descriptions and applicable problem types.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_thresholds
nodes.summary: Returns the configured detection thresholds keyed by problem type.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_set_thresholds
nodes.summary: Persists updated detection thresholds, validating range and emitting an audit entry.

nodes.name: ruvector_healing_problem_types
nodes.summary: Enumerates the problem-type taxonomy understood by the healing engine.

nodes.name: lorentz.rs
nodes.summary: Lorentz (hyperboloid) model of hyperbolic space with Minkowski-dot, distance, and round-trip conversions to/from the Poincare ball used by hyperbolic embedding operators.

nodes.name: LorentzModel
nodes.summary: Marker struct grouping the Lorentz-model operations (dot, distance, Poincare conversions) as associated functions.

nodes.name: minkowski_dot
nodes.summary: Minkowski inner product with signature (-,+,+,...,+) underpinning all Lorentz-model arithmetic.

nodes.name: distance
nodes.summary: Hyperbolic distance between two points on the hyperboloid via arccosh of the negated Minkowski dot.

nodes.name: from_poincare
nodes.summary: Lifts a Poincare-ball vector onto the upper sheet of the Lorentz hyperboloid.

nodes.name: to_poincare
nodes.summary: Projects a hyperboloid point back to the Poincare ball via the standard stereographic map.

nodes.name: parallel.rs
nodes.summary: Parallel scan coordinator for RuHNSW: shared DSM state, partition planning, per-worker scan execution, and tournament-style k-NN result merging used when Postgres opts into a parallel index scan.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors Postgres parallel-scan idioms with explicit shared-memory descriptors and partition coordinators.

nodes.name: RuHnswSharedState
nodes.summary: Shared-memory descriptor coordinating worker progress, partition assignments, and per-worker counters for a parallel HNSW scan.

nodes.name: RuHnswParallelScanDesc
nodes.summary: Per-worker scan descriptor wrapping the shared state plus this worker's identity and progress.

nodes.name: ParallelScanCoordinator
nodes.summary: High-level coordinator that orchestrates worker spawn, partition handoff, and final result reduction for parallel scans.

nodes.name: execute_scan
nodes.summary: Runs the parallel scan from the leader worker, kicking off partition processing and aggregating partial neighbor lists.

nodes.name: scan_partition
nodes.summary: Per-worker HNSW scan over an assigned vector range producing top-k local neighbors.

nodes.name: ruhnsw_estimate_parallel_workers
nodes.summary: Planner-side estimator picking an appropriate worker count based on collection size, ef, and Postgres parallelism GUCs.

nodes.name: merge_knn_results
nodes.summary: Linear k-way merge of per-worker neighbor lists into a single sorted top-k list.

nodes.name: merge_knn_results_tournament
nodes.summary: Tournament-tree variant of the k-way merge optimized for many workers and large k.

nodes.name: execute_parallel_scan
nodes.summary: Entry-point invoked by Postgres' parallel executor to drive the full parallel scan pipeline.

nodes.name: patterns.rs
nodes.summary: Pattern-extraction subsystem that clusters historical query embeddings via centroid updates and converts the clusters into learned routing patterns with confidence/variance metrics.

nodes.name: LearnedPattern
nodes.summary: Recorded query-pattern centroid with optimal-parameter hints (ef, probes) and a confidence score.

nodes.name: PatternExtractor
nodes.summary: Clusters historical queries, derives optimal parameter ranges per cluster, and emits LearnedPattern records consumed by the router.

nodes.name: extract_patterns
nodes.summary: Main pipeline that initializes centroids, iterates k-means style assignments, and produces final LearnedPattern records.

nodes.name: initialize_centroids
nodes.summary: Seeds centroids via k-means++ style probabilistic sampling for stable cluster initialization.

nodes.name: update_centroids
nodes.summary: Recomputes centroids as the mean of assigned points, with empty-cluster handling.

nodes.name: create_patterns
nodes.summary: Converts converged clusters into LearnedPattern rows with derived optimal ef/probes and confidence.

nodes.name: calculate_optimal_ef
nodes.summary: Heuristic that maps a cluster's variance/density to a recommended HNSW ef value.

nodes.name: calculate_optimal_probes
nodes.summary: Heuristic mapping a cluster to an IVF probes count balancing recall and latency.

nodes.name: calculate_confidence
nodes.summary: Combines cluster size and intra-cluster cohesion into a 0-1 confidence score.

nodes.name: calculate_variance
nodes.summary: Computes per-cluster variance used by the ef/probes heuristics.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Internal design doc for the routing module explaining how learned patterns, cost models, and runtime stats steer queries to the optimal index/distance backend.

nodes.name: distance.rs
nodes.summary: Distance kernels for sparse vectors (dot, cosine, euclidean, manhattan, BM25) operating on co-iterated indices/values from the project's sparse vector type.

nodes.name: sparse_dot
nodes.summary: Two-pointer inner product across two sorted sparse vectors.

nodes.name: sparse_cosine
nodes.summary: Cosine distance built on sparse_dot with cached norms for both vectors.

nodes.name: sparse_euclidean
nodes.summary: Sparse L2 distance via merge of index streams, accumulating squared differences for matched and unmatched entries.

nodes.name: sparse_manhattan

nodes.name: sparse_bm25
nodes.summary: BM25 scoring against a sparse query and document representation with configurable k1/b parameters and document-length normalization.

nodes.name: registry.rs
nodes.summary: Multi-tenant registry tracking tenant identities, isolation levels, per-tenant rate limits, lifecycle (register/suspend/resume/delete), and validation hooks used by every SQL entry-point.

nodes.name: IsolationLevel
nodes.summary: Enum of tenant isolation modes (Shared, Schema, Database) governing storage and resource separation.

nodes.name: TenantConfig

nodes.name: TenantSharedState

nodes.name: TenantRegistry

nodes.name: from_json

nodes.name: delete

nodes.name: validate_context

nodes.name: check_promotion
nodes.summary: Evaluates tenant metrics to determine whether the tenant should be promoted to a stricter isolation level.

nodes.name: engine.rs
nodes.summary: RuVector engine background worker — runs a per-database tokio runtime that hosts collection indexes, a query cache, snapshot logic, and an async work queue feeding search/insert/build operations triggered from SQL.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges synchronous Postgres background workers with an internal tokio runtime via a bounded work queue.

nodes.name: EngineWorkerConfig
nodes.summary: Tunable config for the engine worker (cache size, snapshot interval, parallelism, queue depth) loaded from GUCs.

nodes.name: SearchResult
nodes.summary: Tuple-like search result holding a vector id, distance, and optional payload returned from worker queries.

nodes.name: CollectionIndex
nodes.summary: In-memory wrapper around a named collection's HNSW/IVF index with insert/delete/build/update methods.

nodes.name: QueryCache
nodes.summary: Bounded LRU cache mapping query-hash keys to recently returned SearchResult lists.

nodes.name: RuVectorEngine
nodes.summary: Core engine that owns the collection map, query cache, and snapshot machinery and exposes the search/insert/build/update API used by SQL functions and the worker loop.

nodes.name: EngineStats
nodes.summary: Runtime counters (search count, cache hits/misses, snapshot age, queue depth) surfaced via ruvector_engine_stats SQL.

nodes.name: EngineWorker
nodes.summary: Background-worker shell that hosts the engine, drives the run loop, and dispatches queued operations.

nodes.name: put
nodes.summary: QueryCache insertion with LRU eviction and entry-count bookkeeping.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Top-level search API: checks the query cache, performs the index scan, records stats, and caches the result.

nodes.name: perform_search
nodes.summary: Cache-miss path that dispatches the actual ANN search against the chosen collection index.

nodes.name: insert
nodes.summary: Inserts a vector into the named collection, invalidating affected cache entries.

nodes.name: delete
nodes.summary: Removes a vector id from a collection and invalidates dependent cache entries.

nodes.name: build_index
nodes.summary: Builds a brand-new index for a collection, used during CREATE INDEX and rebuild flows.

nodes.name: update_index
nodes.summary: Applies incremental updates to an existing collection index.

nodes.name: snapshot
nodes.summary: Serializes the engine's in-memory state to a durable snapshot (rkyv) for restart recovery.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Worker main loop: polls the work queue, drives snapshot timer, and exits on shutdown signal.

nodes.name: process_work_queue
nodes.summary: Drains the bounded async queue, dispatching each operation to the engine and reporting back to waiters.

nodes.name: process_operation
nodes.summary: Pattern-matches a queued operation variant (search/insert/delete/build/update) and runs the corresponding engine call.

nodes.name: ruvector_engine_worker_main
nodes.summary: pgrx-registered background-worker entry that constructs the EngineWorker and hands control to its run loop.

nodes.name: compute_cache_key
nodes.summary: Hashes (collection, query-vector, k, ef, filter) into a stable cache key for the QueryCache.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Postgres Extension Overview
tour.description: Start with the top-level README for ruvector-postgres to learn what slice 5b builds: a pgrx-based PostgreSQL extension that exposes RuVector as SQL functions, operators, indexes, and access methods. The README outlines the install path, configuration GUCs, supported feature surface (HNSW, IVFFlat, hybrid BM25+vector, graph queries, multi-tenancy, healing) and gives you the vocabulary used throughout the rest of the tour.

tour.title: Slice 5b: pgrx Crate Root & Bootstrap
tour.description: The crate root is the single entry point through which Postgres loads the extension. It declares every feature-gated submodule, registers GUC variables, runs _PG_init at load time, and links into pgrx_embed.rs to satisfy the bgworker linking contract. With 25 outgoing dependencies it acts as the spine that pulls every other slice 5b module into the loaded shared library.
tour.languageLesson: pgrx is the modern Rust framework for PostgreSQL extensions: #[pg_extern] macros generate the C ABI wrappers, GUCs are registered via GucRegistry, and _PG_init runs during shared_preload_libraries load. The companion pgrx_embed binary is required so background workers can find symbols at runtime.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Vector Types & Distance Kernels
tour.description: Building on the bootstrap from Step 2, this layer introduces RuVector's six native Postgres types (vector, halfvec, binaryvec, sparsevec, scalarvec, productvec) plus the distance dispatch table that picks scalar or SIMD L2/cosine/inner-product kernels at runtime. These are the high-fan-in foundations every later layer depends on -- distance/mod.rs has the highest fan-in (10) of the whole slice.
tour.languageLesson: Postgres TOAST handling: large variable-length values use varlena headers and may be compressed or out-of-line. pgrx's PostgresType derive generates in/out/recv/send and typmod functions so Rust structs become first-class SQL types.

tour.title: Slice 5b: HNSW & IVFFlat Access Methods
tour.description: With types and distance in place, the index layer wires HNSW and IVFFlat into Postgres as proper access methods. hnsw.rs implements hierarchical navigable small-world insertion and layer search; ivfflat.rs uses k-means++ partition assignment with parallel probe-based search. The *_am.rs files are the pgrx shims that satisfy the Postgres IndexAmRoutine ABI so CREATE INDEX USING hnsw/ivfflat just works.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Background Workers & IPC
tour.description: Heavy work -- index builds, integrity checks, GNN training, embedding generation -- runs out-of-band in Postgres background workers rather than blocking client backends. workers/mod.rs registers the worker pool; engine.rs hosts a per-database Tokio runtime that owns collection indexes, a query cache, and an async work queue; ipc.rs carries requests from frontend SQL calls to the workers via shared memory.
tour.languageLesson: Postgres background workers (bgworkers) are processes spawned by postmaster and identified by BackgroundWorker entries registered during _PG_init. They communicate with regular backends through shared memory segments and latches rather than function calls, which is why a separate IPC/queue layer is needed.

tour.title: Slice 5b: SPARQL & Property-Graph Engine
tour.description: Slice 5b ships an in-database property graph: a triple store, a SPARQL 1.1 parser/AST/executor with property-path support, and a Cypher front-end. sparql/executor.rs is the heart of the engine -- evaluating BGPs, property paths, joins, GROUP BY, and aggregation against the triple store. The graph subsystem is what lets RuVector serve hybrid vector + graph queries from a single SQL surface.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Hybrid, Sparse & BM25 Search
tour.description: Real-world retrieval combines lexical and semantic signals. The hybrid module orchestrates BM25 keyword scoring against vector similarity using configurable fusion strategies (Reciprocal Rank Fusion, weighted, normalized). It builds on Step 3's vector types and on a CSR-backed sparse vector subsystem for SPLADE-style embeddings, all exposed through SQL register/configure/search/stats functions.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Attention, GNNs & Hyperbolic ML
tour.description: Where most vector databases stop at ANN, slice 5b runs ML directly on stored vectors. The attention module exposes scaled-dot, multi-head, and flash-attention kernels as SQL functions; the GNN module ships GraphSAGE, GCN, and message-passing aggregators; the hyperbolic module adds Poincaré-ball and Lorentz-hyperboloid embeddings for hierarchical data. DAG functions tie these together as composable in-database learning blocks.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Tenancy, RLS & Integrity Gating
tour.description: Multi-tenant deployments need isolation guarantees. tenancy/mod.rs exposes SQL functions for tenant CRUD, quotas, suspension, isolation tiers, and automatic RLS policy generation. The integrity layer pairs this with a Stoer-Wagner min-cut engine that detects weakly-connected partitions in the HNSW graph and uses a hysteresis state machine (gating.rs) to admit, throttle, or block operations under structural stress.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Tiny Dancer Routing & Self-Healing
tour.description: Tiny Dancer is RuVector's learned query router -- a FastGRNN-based agent that picks dense, sparse, or hybrid backends per query based on observed metrics. Pair this with the healing module (HealingEngine + detectors + strategies + a healing worker) and slice 5b becomes self-tuning: degraded indexes are detected, repair strategies are scheduled, and the router learns to avoid hot paths until healing completes.

tour.title: Slice 5b: SQL Install & Upgrade Scripts
tour.description: Postgres extensions are loaded by running .sql install scripts that declare every SQL function, operator, cast, and access method bound to the pgrx C entry points. ruvector--2.0.0.sql is the canonical install for slice 5b -- 230+ functions, the pgvector-compatible ruvector type, operator classes, and AM definitions. The upgrade script bridges between version installations for live deployments.
tour.languageLesson: Postgres extension control: a .control file declares the default version; matching extension--VERSION.sql files declare the SQL surface; extension--FROM--TO.sql files migrate between versions on ALTER EXTENSION UPDATE. Function bodies use LANGUAGE c with AS '$libdir/ruvector', 'symbol_name' to bind to pgrx-generated symbols.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Tests, Benchmarks & Integration
tour.description: Slice 5b's correctness story is anchored by criterion benchmarks (integrity_bench, distance, index build/scan) and pgrx-driven SQL integration tests run across Postgres 14-17. The benchmark workload SQL is itself a high-fan-in artifact -- multiple .sql install scripts pull it in -- and the testing README documents the test matrix that the Docker compose stack in Step 13 exercises.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Docker Multi-PG-Version Stack
tour.description: Operators don't compile extensions by hand. The four-stage Dockerfile produces a Postgres image with the ruvector extension installed (base, dep cache, extension build, minimal runtime) while docker-compose.yml fans out across Postgres 14, 15, 16, and 17 test/benchmark/dev containers. Together they package every piece introduced in Steps 2-11 into a reproducible deployment artifact.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds with a separate dependency-cache stage are the standard pattern for Rust extensions: cargo's slow dependency compile gets cached in its own layer, the extension build copies only sources, and the final runtime stage drops cargo and toolchains entirely -- shrinking image size while keeping rebuilds fast.

tour.title: Slice 5b: Cluster Coordinator (Distributed RuVector)
tour.description: The final stop in slice 5b is the sister crate ruvector-cluster -- the distributed coordinator that turns single-node extensions from Steps 2-13 into a multi-node deployment. lib.rs exposes the ClusterManager API; discovery.rs offers static/gossip/multicast membership; shard.rs implements consistent-hashing and jump-hash routing with migration tracking; consensus.rs runs a DAG-based vertex-submission engine with tip selection, vector-clock dominance, and conflict detection. This is how RuVector scales horizontally beyond one Postgres instance.
